articles.summary: The Income-Tax Department has sent notices to 63,000 restaurants after AI tools detected suppression of sales and under-reporting of income. Nationwide surveys revealed nearly ₹408 crore in suppressed sales. Eateries have been requested to update their tax returns by March 31 to avoid penalties, as the department continues its crackdown on tax evasion in the F&B sector.

articles.headline: TCS Market Capitalization Drops Below 100 Billion Dollars From Peak
articles.summary: Tata Consultancy Services (TCS) has seen its market capitalization fall by nearly $100 billion from its all-time high valuation of $200.15 billion reached in January 2022. On Tuesday, the company's market cap stood at approximately $99 billion (Rs 9.09 lakh crore), reflecting a significant decline in investor wealth amid global uncertainty in the IT sector.

articles.headline: Kolkata Leather Hub Faces Uncertainty Over US Tariffs and Global Conflicts
articles.summary: The leather industry in Kolkata is struggling as the US White House imposed a 10% surcharge following a court ruling against previous tariff orders. Additionally, the West Asia crisis has made exports to the US via Europe logistically difficult. Exporters are currently holding shipments while awaiting clarity on the final tariff rates, which could reach 15%.

articles.headline: Restaurant Bills Expected to Rise by 30 Percent Due to LPG Crisis
articles.summary: Volatility in raw material prices and a shortage of commercial LPG cylinders are expected to make dining out significantly more expensive. Industry officials suggest restaurant prices could increase by 20 to 30 percent as early as April. The cost of commercial LPG cylinders has already reached Rs 1,883 in Delhi, forcing businesses to pass the burden to consumers.

articles.headline: Auto Component Makers Seek Government Help Amidst Fuel Supply Shortages
articles.summary: The Automotive Component Manufacturers Association (ACMA) has requested assistance from the Ministry of Heavy Industries regarding logistics costs and fuel availability. Rising tensions in West Asia and disruptions in the Red Sea have increased export costs by 20-40%. The industry is concerned about the consistent supply of LPG and PNG required for manufacturing processes.

articles.headline: Emami Agrotech Targets 2000 Crore Revenue in Food Services and B2B
articles.summary: Emami Agrotech, a part of the Emami Group, is expanding its presence in the Hotel, Restaurant, and Catering (HORECA) and B2B food services segments. The company launched its "For Chef" range at the AAHAR 2026 fair in New Delhi. It aims to achieve a turnover of Rs 2,000 crore in this category within the next two to three years through a diversified product portfolio.

articles.headline: Renault Announces India Strategy with Plans for Four New Models by 2030
articles.summary: French automaker Renault has unveiled its "Future-Ready" strategic plan for the Indian market. The company identifies India as one of its three key global growth hubs. Renault plans to launch four new models in India by 2030, including electric and hybrid versions based on its modular platforms, aiming for 2 million global unit sales by the end of the decade.

articles.headline: Akasa Air Announces 25 Lakh Rupee Retention Incentive for Pilots
articles.summary: To retain flight crew amid intense competition in the aviation sector, Akasa Air has introduced a long-term incentive plan. Captains and Training Captains are eligible for a payment of up to Rs 25 lakh, to be paid in three installments through August 2029. The plan aims to secure experienced cockpit crew as the airline expands its fleet, which currently includes 35 Boeing 737 MAX aircraft.

articles.headline: Cricketers Suryakumar Yadav and Tilak Varma Invest in Quzhou Solar
articles.summary: Indian cricket stars Suryakumar Yadav and Tilak Varma have announced investments in the Hyderabad-based renewable energy company Quzhou Solar. The company provides solar energy solutions and operates under Suntek Energy Systems. The investment by the T20 World Cup winners is expected to strengthen the company’s position in India’s clean energy sector.

articles.headline: LIC Portfolio Loses 70,105 Crore Rupees Due to West Asia Conflict
articles.summary: The Life Insurance Corporation of India (LIC) has incurred a loss of Rs 70,105 crore in its investment portfolio due to the ongoing US-Israel-Iran conflict. Banking stocks and Larsen & Toubro (L&T) were the hardest hit. L&T's main income could drop by 11-12% in FY27/28 due to project delays and reduced orders in the West Asia region, where it has significant hydrocarbon operations.

articles.headline: Government Considers New Insurance Scheme to Support Exporters Amidst Crisis
articles.summary: Union Commerce Minister Piyush Goyal stated that the government is exploring a new insurance scheme to aid exporters affected by the West Asia conflict. The ministry is working with the Export Credit Guarantee Corporation (ECGC) to develop relief measures. Goyal emphasized the need for India to become a top global exporter in agriculture and clean energy sectors.

articles.headline: Eateries Curtail Menus as LPG Shortage Hits Commercial Kitchens
articles.summary: Restaurants and food outlets across India are facing a severe shortage of commercial LPG cylinders due to supply disruptions from the Gulf. The National Restaurant Association of India has advised members to consider shorter operating hours and reduced menus. Some establishments in Bengaluru and Mumbai have already closed or switched to induction cooking to conserve fuel.

articles.headline: RBI Liberalises Bank Dividend Norms for Commercial Banks
articles.summary: The Reserve Bank of India has issued revised guidelines giving commercial banks more flexibility to pay higher dividends. Banks with a Net NPA ratio of less than 7% can now pay dividends from net profits. The maximum payout ratio is capped at 50%, provided the bank meets the minimum capital adequacy requirements. The rules aim to reward shareholders while maintaining financial stability.

articles.headline: NIIF Raises $750 Million for Second Private Market Fund
articles.summary: The National Investment and Infrastructure Fund (NIIF) has raised $750 million for its second private markets fund. Investors include the India-UAE investment venture and the Asian Infrastructure Investment Bank. The fund will target mid-market growth companies in India across sectors like technology, healthcare, and consumer services, aiming to provide institutional capital.

articles.headline: Restaurant industry raises alarm over commercial LPG cylinder supply confusion
articles.summary: A government order prioritizing domestic LPG use has caused confusion among distributors, leading to fears of commercial cylinder shortages for eateries. National restaurant associations are seeking urgent clarification to ensure uninterrupted supply. Currently, 80-90% of Indian restaurants depend on LPG cylinders as piped gas availability remains scarce in most urban centers.

articles.headline: Investors turn to office REITs as defensive assets amid war volatility
articles.summary: The West Asian crisis has halted an IT-sector-led sell-off in domestic office real estate investment trusts (REITs). Investors are flocking to these defensive, income-generating assets, which offer annual yields of 6-7% and serve as a safe haven during geopolitical uncertainty. Despite weak discretionary tech spending, REIT occupancy remains stable at roughly 93% across major Indian cities.

articles.headline: India’s API exports surpass imports to reach ₹41,500 crore in FY25
articles.summary: Chemicals and fertilizers minister J.P. Nadda informed the Rajya Sabha that exports of active pharmaceutical ingredients (APIs) exceeded total imports, which stood at ₹39,215 crore. The government has implemented various measures over the last 11 years to boost domestic production and reduce dependence on foreign drug components, strengthening the country's pharma self-reliance.

articles.headline: RBI softens proposed dividend payout framework for Indian banks
articles.summary: The Reserve Bank of India modified rules regarding how banks treat bad loans when calculating profits for dividends. Banks will now deduct only 50% of net non-performing assets instead of the full amount, following industry feedback. The adjustment is intended to provide banks with more flexibility in dividend distribution while maintaining financial stability.

articles.headline: Small steelmakers face production cuts due to West Asia gas disruption
articles.summary: Indian steel producers have warned of output reductions as the conflict in the Middle East disrupts gas supplies. Most gas producers declared force majeure last week, restricting supply to industries. The shortage of LNG and rising energy costs are impacting the operations of smaller mills that rely on gas-based manufacturing for alloy production.

articles.headline: Aluminium prices surge as West Asia conflict disrupts global supply
articles.summary: Shares of National Aluminium Co. (Nalco) rose as aluminium spot prices hit $3,400 per tonne on the London Metal Exchange, the highest since 2022. Supply concerns grew after major producers in Bahrain and Qatar declared force majeure or suspended operations due to gas shortages and shipping disruptions through the Strait of Hormuz, which accounts for 9% of global output.

articles.headline: Crude oil derivative volumes on MCX triple amid price volatility
articles.summary: Trading in crude oil derivative contracts on the Multi Commodity Exchange jumped 2.8x as the Iran-US war triggered sharp price swings. Notional turnover reached ₹1.15 trillion over six sessions. Traders are rushing to capitalize on heightened volatility as several West Asian producers reduced output, pushing global oil benchmarks toward $120 per barrel before settling lower.

articles.headline: AU Small Finance Bank moves closer to universal bank transition
articles.summary: The RBI relaxed non-operative financial holding company requirements, removing a major structural hurdle for AU Small Finance Bank's transition to a universal bank. The shift will provide greater flexibility in lending, reduce the cost of deposits, and allow the bank to expand into larger-ticket loans, improving overall return metrics and profitability.

articles.headline: Auto component industry seeks government support amid Iran war disruptions
articles.summary: India’s auto parts exporters have approached the government for financial aid as logistics costs jumped 20-40% due to vessel rerouting around the Cape of Good Hope. The industry is also facing delays in importing critical raw materials like synthetic rubber and aluminium scrap. ACMA has requested temporary working capital support and assured energy access to offset cash-flow stress.

articles.headline: State Bank of India to issue infrastructure bonds in March
articles.summary: India’s largest lender plans to raise up to ₹10,000 crore through 7-year or 10-year infrastructure bonds after a 16-month gap. The funds will be used to finance long-term development projects. This follows similar bond sales by Bank of Baroda earlier this month, as state-run lenders look to tap the market for long-term financing amid resilient domestic demand.

articles.headline: Boeing 737 MAX deliveries face delays due to wiring flaws
articles.summary: Boeing reported that first-quarter deliveries of its 737 MAX jets could be delayed as the company performs rework to fix scratched wires caused by a machining error. The planemaker stated that in-service aircraft remain safe and the issue will not impact its year-end delivery targets. Boeing delivered 51 jets in February, its highest for the month since 2018.

articles.headline: Middle East airlines pause aircraft deals as fuel costs spike
articles.summary: Gulf carriers and Asian airlines are reviewing growth plans and pausing aircraft deliveries due to the war in West Asia. Rising jet fuel prices and operational difficulties have led some carriers to consider invoking force majeure clauses. Boeing is especially exposed as roughly 14% of its backlog and half of its widebody order book comes from Middle Eastern airlines.

articles.headline: Gujarat emerges as India's leading semiconductor manufacturing hub
articles.summary: Gujarat has become the first state in India to introduce a dedicated Semiconductor Policy, attracting major global players like Micron. The state has secured nearly 40% of India's approved semiconductor manufacturing capacity, with significant investments in Sanand and Dholera. The move is expected to create thousands of high-tech jobs and boost India's electronics exports.

articles.headline: Restaurants trim menus and hours as commercial LPG shortage bites
articles.summary: A severe shortage of commercial LPG caused by West Asia disruptions is forcing restaurants nationwide to shrink menus and operating hours. Industry bodies like the NRAI have advised members to introduced limited “crisis menus” and fast-cooking items. In cities like Mumbai, nearly 50% of establishments fear temporary closure if gas supplies are not restored urgently.

articles.headline: Government forms panel to manage LPG supply for hospitality sector
articles.summary: The Ministry of Petroleum and Natural Gas has formed a three-member committee of oil marketing executives to address the LPG supply crunch affecting restaurants and hotels. While domestic household supply remains the government's top priority, the panel will review representations from essential non-domestic sectors and prioritize stock allocation based on availability.

articles.headline: Auto industry seeks restoration of gas supply to prevent production halts
articles.summary: Automotive bodies SIAM and ACMA have requested the government to restore LPG and PNG allocations for industrial production. Manufacturers warn that ongoing supply disruptions in hubs like Gujarat could stall vehicle production and impact MSME component suppliers. Geopolitical instability has already led to a 50% cut in industrial gas usage in some regions.

articles.headline: Food makers and exporters report rising packaging costs due to volatility
articles.summary: FMCG firms and rice exporters are facing a 15-20% surge in packaging costs as crude oil volatility impacts polymer derivatives. Industry players warn that if current trends persist, they will be forced to pass these costs to consumers. Exporters have urged the government to allow temporary zero-duty imports of polymer granules to mitigate the price pressure.

articles.headline: Iran war shadow looms over Kashmir handicraft export trade
articles.summary: Kashmir's handicraft exporters fear significant losses as the West Asia conflict threatens shipping routes and buyer demand. The sector supports 4.45 lakh artisans who produce pashmina shawls and silk carpets. Geopolitical tensions are expected to lead to higher freight costs and insurance premiums, mirroring disruptions seen during the earlier Russia-Ukraine war.

articles.headline: Proposed RBI reform to allow direct bank lending to REITs
articles.summary: An RBI proposal to permit banks to lend directly to Real Estate Investment Trusts is expected to accelerate capital formation in the sector. Experts suggest that to be successful, bank lending must be treated as an income-yield credit product rather than project finance. The move is seen as a sign of regulatory confidence in India's maturing real estate capital markets.

articles.headline: Fertilizer stocks rally following government regulations on gas supply
articles.summary: Shares of fertilizer companies surged up to 20% after the government prioritized natural gas supply for the sector through the Essential Commodities Act. While the rally boosted stocks like FACT and RCF, analysts caution that rising ammonia and LNG prices could eventually hurt profitability unless nutrient-based subsidy rates are revised for the upcoming season.

articles.headline: Public sector banks earned ₹28,646 crore from third-party product sales
articles.summary: Finance Ministry data reveals that public sector banks earned over ₹28,000 crore in five years by selling insurance, mutual funds, and other third-party products. State Bank of India was the highest earner in this category. Finance Minister Nirmala Sitharaman has previously criticized banks for spending excessive time on insurance sales rather than core lending activities.

articles.headline: FMCG firms step up acquisitions of direct-to-consumer start-ups
articles.summary: Large consumer goods companies like HUL, ITC, and Marico are increasingly acquiring digital-first brands to strengthen their presence in emerging categories. This trend is creating clearer exit pathways for venture capital and private equity investors. Strategic buyers are prioritizing scalable brands with strong repeat customer cohorts and disciplined operations.

articles.headline: TVS Supply Chain establishes new global sourcing unit for Caterpillar
articles.summary: TVS Supply Chain Solutions has opened a 40,000 sq ft warehousing facility at the Free Trade and Warehousing Zone near Chennai. The unit will manage global parts procurement for Caterpillar’s manufacturing operations, aiming to optimize lead times and landed costs. The project reinforces the 'Make in India' initiative and utilizes Chennai's industrial corridor connectivity.

articles.headline: Blue Star expects thirty percent growth despite rising energy standards
articles.summary: Air-conditioning firm Blue Star is targeting 30% value growth in FY26, even as energy efficiency standards are expected to push prices up by 13-15%. The company plans to invest ₹140 crore in R&D and marketing to increase its market share. However, management flagged risks from West Asia disruptions affecting essential manufacturing inputs like LNG and PNG.

articles.headline: Hero MotoCorp to increase investment in electric vehicle maker Euler Motors
articles.summary: Hero MotoCorp will invest an additional ₹275 crore in Euler Motors, raising its stake in the electric commercial vehicle start-up to approximately 36%. Euler plans to use the capital to double its production capacity to 2,000 vehicles per month. The start-up currently holds a 20% share in the electric four-wheel cargo segment as urban freight demand grows.

articles.headline: Kirloskar Oil Engines announces leadership transition and succession plan
articles.summary: Kirloskar Oil Engines Ltd has appointed Rahul Kirloskar as Chairperson and Gauri Kirloskar as Vice-Chairperson, effective April 2026. The move follows the planned retirement of Atul Kirloskar, who will step down after serving the company for more than four decades. The company stated the timely transition would allow the new leadership team to advance its future vision.

articles.headline: Women users lead growth in premium smartphone resale at Cashify
articles.summary: Re-commerce platform Cashify reported that women subscribers grew twice as fast as men last year, showing a strong preference for premium devices like the iPhone 15 and 16. Women now make up nearly 20% of the platform's user base. The trend indicates a shift toward active device lifecycle management and financial independence among women in metro cities.

articles.headline: LPG supply concerns trigger surge in demand for induction cooktops
articles.summary: Indian consumers are flocking to purchase induction stoves as an alternative to LPG amid West Asia supply fears. E-commerce platforms reported sales surges of up to 20 times normal daily volumes, with several quick commerce apps running out of stock. Manufacturers have seen weekly sales quadruple as households look for electrical cooking utensils to offset potential gas shortages.

articles.headline: Epson India targets industrial printing to drive future revenue growth
articles.summary: Epson India is recalibrating its business to focus on digital textile and label printing, expecting these segments to contribute up to 12% of revenue within four years. The company is positioning digital printing as a sustainable alternative to conventional processes that use high amounts of water. Epson currently holds over 50% market share in India's ink tank printer category.

articles.headline: Anthropic Sues US Government Over Supply Chain Risk Label
articles.summary: AI startup Anthropic is suing the Trump administration after the Pentagon designated it a "supply chain risk." The company claims the label was an unlawful punishment for its refusal to allow unrestricted military use of its technology. Anthropic is the first US firm to receive such a designation, which is typically reserved for foreign adversary companies like Huawei.

articles.headline: Karnataka Government Plans to Regulate Social Media Use for Minors
articles.summary: Karnataka's Minister for IT and BT, Priyank Kharge, stated that the state government aims to regulate social media use for children under 16. The goal is to protect minors from harmful content and exploitation due to excessive screen time. The government plans to consult stakeholders to develop guidelines within the next five months to ensure responsible digital usage.

articles.headline: Social Media Firms Get Time for AI Content Labelling Audit
articles.summary: The Indian government will provide social media platforms more time to comply with new regulations requiring the identification and labelling of AI-generated content. Platforms with over 5 million users will need to deploy automated tools to verify such content and maintain digital logs. The move follows concerns from tech giants regarding the technical readiness of the ecosystem.

articles.headline: Cybersecurity Talent Gap Leaves India Inc Vulnerable to Attacks
articles.summary: India is facing a critical shortage of cybersecurity professionals, with only 380,000 experts against a demand for 1.2 million. A report by Forrester Research suggests that 85% of Indian companies lack sufficient security staff to handle complex threats. The talent gap is particularly acute in senior leadership and specialized roles like cloud security and incident response.

articles.headline: Prime Minister Modi emphasizes growth in animation and gaming sectors
articles.summary: Prime Minister Narendra Modi stated that India is actively promoting the Animation, Visual Effects, Gaming, and Comics (AVGC) sector as part of its transition toward an innovation-driven economy. He encouraged educational institutions to strengthen industry collaboration and research-driven learning to support the growth of this emerging digital industry.

articles.headline: One-third of laptops sold in India now locally assembled
articles.summary: Government production-linked incentives (PLI) have pushed electronics manufacturers to increase local assembly. In March 2024, less than 5% of laptops were made in India; that figure has grown to nearly 33% as global brands like HP, Asus, and Lenovo utilize local manufacturing partners. The shift is part of a broader strategy to diversify electronics revenue away from mobile phones.

articles.headline: Amazon targets $37 billion bond offering to fund AI infrastructure
articles.summary: Amazon.com Inc. has launched one of the largest corporate bond sales ever to finance investments in artificial intelligence infrastructure. The tech giant seeks to raise between $37 billion and $42 billion in a multi-tranche offering across dollar and euro markets. The sale is part of a trend of jumbo note offerings by hyperscalers planning massive AI infrastructure investments.

articles.headline: Meta to charge location fees to cover digital service taxes
articles.summary: Meta Platforms informed advertisers it will begin charging "location fees" on July 1 to cover digital services taxes imposed by various countries. The extra charges will apply to ads delivered in specific jurisdictions, even if the advertiser is not based there. The move mirrors similar fee adjustments implemented by other tech giants like Google and Amazon.

articles.headline: SpaceX considers Nasdaq listing for potentially record-breaking IPO
articles.summary: Elon Musk’s SpaceX is leaning toward listing shares on the Nasdaq for what could be the largest initial public offering ever. The company is seeking inclusion in the Nasdaq 100 index as a condition for the listing, though final decisions on the exchange are pending. The IPO could launch as early as June, though plans remain confidential and subject to change.

articles.headline: IndiaMART sues OpenAI over exclusion from ChatGPT search results
articles.summary: B2B marketplace IndiaMART has filed a lawsuit against OpenAI, alleging ChatGPT discriminates by excluding its listings while showing competitors. OpenAI cited IndiaMART's appearance in US "notorious market" reports for counterfeit pharmaceuticals and electronics as a reason. IndiaMART claims the exclusion has a "catastrophic" impact on millions of small business suppliers.

articles.headline: Apple launches budget-friendly MacBook Neo amid global chip shortage
articles.summary: Apple has introduced the MacBook Neo, priced at $599, its least expensive laptop ever. The move leverages older hardware components to navigate high memory chip costs and capture budget-conscious consumers as global laptop shipments decline. The strategy targets the entry-level segment currently dominated by Google’s Chromebook, offering a premium brand at a lower price point.

articles.headline: India must use data leverage to secure access to AI models
articles.summary: Experts argue that India should make access to its vast datasets conditional on AI companies depositing model weights in local facilities. This "escrow" model would protect India's technological sovereignty if access to foreign AI APIs is cut off during future geopolitical conflicts. Leveraging its large user base is seen as a strategic currency in the emerging AI era.

articles.headline: AI used by Iranian-linked hackers to target critical infrastructure
articles.summary: A report by cybersecurity firm CloudSEK reveals that Iranian-linked hacking groups are using generative AI to conduct more sophisticated attacks on critical infrastructure. The groups have targeted power grids, water plants, and financial systems in several countries. The use of AI allows them to automate vulnerability scanning and create more convincing phishing campaigns.

articles.headline: Women leaders are shaping the future of AI in India
articles.summary: A feature on 'Times Techies' highlights the growing role of women in leadership positions within India's artificial intelligence sector. From software development to ethical governance, women are leading major initiatives at top firms like Infosys, Cognizant, and IBM. The article emphasizes the importance of diverse perspectives in building unbiased and transparent AI systems.

articles.headline: Electronics and semiconductor supply chains remain sensitive to global shocks
articles.summary: While India's electronics sector has currently avoided major disruptions from the West Asia conflict, industry players warn of risks if instability persists. Logistics costs for components are rising, and gas required for semiconductor manufacturing remains vulnerable. Analysts state the turmoil serves as a catalyst for India to strengthen domestic chip design and manufacturing capabilities.

articles.headline: States explore regulations to restrict social media access for minors
articles.summary: Andhra Pradesh and Karnataka are considering time-bound regulations to limit social media access for children due to concerns over digital addiction and mental health. While the tech industry has been slow to address these risks, experts call for a coordinated national framework to enforce teen-safe platform designs, transparency in algorithms, and default youth privacy settings.

articles.headline: AI platform FarMart reports significant reduction in post-harvest food loss
articles.summary: AI-native agri platform FarMart prevented over 18,000 tonnes of post-harvest food loss and saved 5.39 billion litres of freshwater in 2025. The company's impact report shows its technology-led supply chain redesigned coordination across 4.87 lakh farmers. By optimizing logistics and payment cycles, the platform aims to reduce income volatility and carbon emissions in the agri-value chain.

articles.headline: Nuclear energy must grow to support AI digital infrastructure demands
articles.summary: Infosys co-founder Kris Gopalakrishnan stated that nuclear energy's share of India's energy mix must rise from 1.7% to 20% to support the power-intensive infrastructure required for the AI era. He noted that AI could raise industrial productivity by 30% and called for the retraining of India's 5.5 million IT professionals to become proficient in using and developing AI systems.

articles.headline: CBSE Investigates Mathematical Paper QR Code Rickroll Incident on Social Media
articles.summary: The Central Board of Secondary Education (CBSE) is reviewing its processes after students reported that a QR code on the Class 12 Mathematics exam paper linked to a "Rickroll" meme. CBSE clarified that the question papers were authentic and their security was not compromised. Rickrolling is a long-standing internet prank that redirects users to a specific music video.

articles.headline: Karnataka government cuts 1st PU pass marks from 35 to 33
articles.summary: The Karnataka government has officially reduced the passing marks for first-year Pre-University (PU) examinations from 35 to 33. The decision aims to bring parity with other boards like CBSE and SSLC. The change will be reflected in the Karnataka Pre-University Examination Rules, 2025, affecting approximately 6 lakh students.

articles.headline: CBSE maths paper QR code 'Rickrolls' students instead of providing solutions
articles.summary: Students appearing for the CBSE Class XII Mathematics exam were in for a surprise when a QR code intended for additional resources led to the music video of Rick Astley’s 'Never Gonna Give You Up.' CBSE termed it an 'inadvertent error' by a technician and assured students that steps are being taken to prevent such pranks in the future.

articles.headline: Government Seeks Private Sector Inputs for Deploying Bharat Small Reactors
articles.summary: The Indian government has invited expressions of interest from private companies for the deployment of Bharat Small Reactors (BSRs). The move aims to integrate small modular nuclear reactors into the country's energy mix for captive power consumption. The nuclear facility would be operated by state-run NPCIL, while the industrial user would bear the project funding.

articles.headline: India to validate indigenous AI weather forecasting model after monsoon
articles.summary: The India Meteorological Department plans to evaluate its AI-based weather model by comparing predictions with actual rainfall data after the 2026 monsoon. If successful, the high-resolution system, which operates on a 6-km grid, will improve disaster preparedness and agricultural planning by detecting complex patterns like El Niño and sea surface temperature changes more accurately.

articles.headline: IISc researchers develop chip to secure IoT devices from quantum threats
articles.summary: Researchers at the Indian Institute of Science (IISc) have developed a low-power, compact chip designed to protect Internet of Things (IoT) devices from future quantum computer attacks. The chip uses 'quantum-safe' digital signatures to ensure that data sent by connected devices cannot be intercepted or altered, even by highly advanced computers.

articles.headline: Camel immune system offers potential new dengue antidote
articles.summary: Scientists at IISER Mohali have discovered that nanobodies derived from camels can neutralize all four strains of the dengue virus. These nanobodies are smaller and more stable than human antibodies, making them more effective at preventing the virus from entering cells. The discovery could lead to a universal treatment for dengue, which affects millions in India.

articles.headline: SAT Upholds SEBI Order Against Sahara India Real Estate Corporation
articles.summary: The Securities Appellate Tribunal (SAT) has upheld SEBI's regulatory action against Sahara India Commercial Corporation (SICCL). The tribunal dismissed appeals regarding the illegal issuance of Optional Fully Convertible Debentures (OFCDs). SICCL had raised approximately Rs 14,106 crore from 1.98 crore investors between 1998 and 2008, which the court deemed a public offering.

articles.headline: IDFC First Bank Settles 645 Crore Rupees in Chandigarh Branch Fraud
articles.summary: IDFC First Bank has settled claims worth Rs 645 crore with the Haryana government following a fraud incident at its Chandigarh branch. The bank paid an additional Rs 55 crore after initial estimates were revised. The case involved bank employees using fraudulent methods to approve investments. The bank stated that all parking accounts at the branch have now been resolved.

articles.headline: Assam Government Transfers Rs 9,000 Each to 40 Lakh Women
articles.summary: The Assam government has distributed Rs 3,600 crore through direct benefit transfers to 40 lakh women under the Orunodoi scheme. Each beneficiary received Rs 9,000, which includes four months of financial assistance and a festival bonus. Chief Minister Himanta Biswa Sarma stated the coverage of the scheme has reached 4 million women, aimed at providing financial security.

articles.headline: Government Seeks Parliament Nod for Rs 2.81 Lakh Crore Extra Spending
articles.summary: The Union government has sought parliamentary approval for additional gross expenditure of Rs 2.81 lakh crore for the current fiscal year. The supplementary demand for grants includes higher allocations for food and fertilizer subsidies, defence services, and transfers to an Economic Stabilisation Fund. Finance Ministry officials ruled out any immediate risk of fiscal slippage.

articles.headline: IndiGo CEO Pieter Elbers resigns as Rahul Bhatia takes interim charge
articles.summary: Pieter Elbers, CEO of InterGlobe Aviation (IndiGo), has resigned with immediate effect. Co-founder and Managing Director Rahul Bhatia will assume interim management until a permanent successor is named. Elbers led the airline through a period of international expansion and massive aircraft orders, but his departure follows recent regulatory scrutiny over widespread flight disruptions.

articles.headline: Air India to levy fuel surcharge on domestic and international tickets
articles.summary: Air India has announced it will start levying a fuel surcharge on all tickets from March 12 due to rising jet fuel prices. Domestic flights will see a surcharge of ₹399, while international routes will have surcharges ranging from $10 to $50 depending on the destination. Other major Indian carriers are expected to follow suit as operational costs rise.

articles.headline: Equity mutual fund inflows jump 8% to ₹25,977 crore in February
articles.summary: Data from the Association of Mutual Funds in India shows a rise in equity appetite, led by mid- and small-cap funds. This suggests a shift in retail investor preference toward high-growth potential stocks despite a flat market. However, the data does not yet capture the impact of the Gulf conflict that began on February 28, which has since rattled global equities.
